MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Fee paid. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion To Compel X-Complainants Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrgoatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-COMPLAINANT AM PM LOGISTICS' FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Marc A. Centor, Esq. of Cox, Wootton, Griffin, Hansen & Poulos, LLP 438-4600 for x-deft & moving party; Larry R. Shockey, Esq. of The Law Office of Larry R. Shockey 584-0134 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties AM PM Logistics & Jonathan Harris.

RULING: Moving party provided an update from the previous hearing and indicated that he has received supplemental responses that are more complete. Therefore, the Court permanently stayed the additional $2,000.00 sanctions award. The original $2,000.00 sanctions is due 08/31/11. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

Notice Of Motion To Compel X-Complainants Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrgoatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Continued To Jul-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion To Compel X-Complainants Further Responses To Requests For Production And Special Interrgoatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-01-2011 Continued To Jul-21-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT FREEMAN DECORATING SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL CROSS-COMPLAINANT AM PM LOGISTICS' FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND FOR SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Marc A. Centor, Esq. of Cox, Wootton, Griffin, Hansen & Poulos, LLP 438-4600 for x-deft & moving party; Larry R. Shockey, Esq. of The Law Office of Larry R. Shockey 584-0134 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties AM PM Logistics & Jonathan Harris.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL granted, responses to be provided by 07/20/11. SANCTIONS GRANTED in the amount of $2,000.00 to be divided by Mr. Shockey and his client to be paid to cross-defendant. Court granted an additional $2,000.00 in sanctions, however stayed it until 07/21/11 pending compliance. Matter continued to 07/21/11. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
